data.reasoningText:
The .sln file seems to not actually be triggering builds of the individual projects, even though `dotnet sln list` shows them. This is a .NET SDK 10 environment. Let me check the sln file more carefully. The issue might be with the GUIDs in the solution file - the project GUIDs have non-standard formats (like `{C1111111-...}`). But that should still work. 

Wait, looking at the output - "Rebuild target(s)" completes instantly without building any projects. The solution's `ValidateSolutionConfiguration` runs but doesn't actually build the projects. This could be a solution configuration issue - the projects aren't mapped to solution configurations.
 
I think I found it - the solution file is missing the `ProjectConfigurationPlatforms` section entirely. Without that mapping in the Global section, MSBuild has no idea which projects should build under each configuration, so nothing actually gets built even though the projects are listed.
